response.text:
REJECT

rationale: My policy requires 50% SoC threshold before sharing, but r2c0's offer leaves them at only 46% SoC—below my conservative threshold—creating mutual depletion risk during an 11.5-hour outage.

system: You are the reactive subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Be brief and decisive.
user:
You are reacting to a OFFER from r2c0. Payload: {'kwh': 0.625}. Their rationale: SoC 17.96/39 kWh (0.46 frac) above 0.25 threshold; sharing 2.50 kW via geographic circle to below-mean-SoC peer..
Your current policy: sharing_intent=conservative, share_min_soc_frac=0.5, distrusted_peers=[].
Your latest belief: Neighbors are willing to share; I should conserve my battery and only share when well above 50% SoC to survive the remaining 11.5 hours of outage..
Reply with one of ACCEPT / REJECT / COUNTER on the first line, followed by `rationale: <one sentence>`.
